songs.title: Welcome Home
songs.scaleHint: Eb minor / F# major area; for solo play, use Eb minor pentatonic. If your guitar is in standard, think E minor shapes sounding Eb if track is tuned down.
songs.mustKnow: Iconic intro/main riff and heavy rhythmic hits.
songs.fallback: Simplified down-tuned power chords; skip fast lead detail.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Are we tuned down a half-step, and am I covering the intro riff or rhythm support?

source.Scale Hint: Eb minor / F# major area; for solo play, use Eb minor pentatonic. If your guitar is in standard, think E minor shapes sounding Eb if track is tuned down.
source.Must Know: Iconic intro/main riff and heavy rhythmic hits.
source.Fallback: Simplified down-tuned power chords; skip fast lead detail.
source.Rehearsal Question: Are we tuned down a half-step, and am I covering the intro riff or rhythm support?

songs.title: All The Small Things
songs.scaleHint: C major / A minor area; improv not the priority.
songs.mustKnow: Downstroke/power-chord stamina and stops.
songs.fallback: Root-fifth power chords with locked eighth notes.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Do we play it like the record or shorten any repeats?

source.Scale Hint: C major / A minor area; improv not the priority.
source.Must Know: Downstroke/power-chord stamina and stops.
source.Fallback: Root-fifth power chords with locked eighth notes.
source.Rehearsal Question: Do we play it like the record or shorten any repeats?

songs.title: A Little Less Conversation
songs.practiceStyle: Some improv, but learn key hooks
songs.scaleHint: E minor/E blues often works for the common guitar arrangement; keep fills short if singing.
songs.mustKnow: Groove, vocal entrances, and clean chord rhythm.
songs.fallback: Dry clean/edge-of-breakup rhythm only.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Am I singing it, playing it, or both?

source.Rehearsal Question: Am I singing it, playing it, or both?

songs.title: I Believe in a Thing Called Love
songs.scaleHint: E major / C# minor pentatonic family for much of the rock vocabulary; parts are more important than noodling.
songs.mustKnow: Intro/rhythm riffs and big chorus changes.
songs.fallback: Chugging rhythm chords; leave lead gymnastics to others.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Which guitar part do they need: rhythm, harmonized leads, or just texture?

source.Track: I Believe in a Thing Called Love

source.Fallback: Chugging rhythm chords; leave lead gymnastics to others.
source.Rehearsal Question: Which guitar part do they need: rhythm, harmonized leads, or just texture?

songs.title: Thunderstruck
songs.scaleHint: B minor pentatonic for lead ideas, but intro precision is everything.
songs.mustKnow: Intro riff timing; no sloppy faking.
songs.fallback: Malcolm-style rhythm on the chorus sections.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Who owns the intro? Can I just reinforce rhythm?

source.Scale Hint: B minor pentatonic for lead ideas, but intro precision is everything.
source.Must Know: Intro riff timing; no sloppy faking.
source.Fallback: Malcolm-style rhythm on the chorus sections.
source.Rehearsal Question: Who owns the intro? Can I just reinforce rhythm?

songs.title: Don't Stop Believin'
songs.practiceStyle: Some improv, but learn key hooks
songs.linkQuality: Good guitar backing track with vocals
songs.scaleHint: E major / C# minor pentatonic for melodic fills.
songs.mustKnow: Section entrances and chorus dynamics.
songs.fallback: Clean rhythm/chord support; skip Neal Schon lead details.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Are keys covering most of it? Where do they want guitar big?

songs.title: Tribute
songs.scaleHint: A minor pentatonic for comedy-rock solo section, but rhythmic acoustic part matters more.
songs.mustKnow: Acoustic pattern and dynamic story beats.
songs.fallback: Strum chord skeleton and follow vocals.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Do they want the album arrangement or a loose acoustic version?

source.Scale Hint: A minor pentatonic for comedy-rock solo section, but rhythmic acoustic part matters more.
source.Must Know: Acoustic pattern and dynamic story beats.
source.Fallback: Strum chord skeleton and follow vocals.
source.Rehearsal Question: Do they want the album arrangement or a loose acoustic version?

songs.title: While My Guitar Gently Weeps
songs.scaleHint: A minor pentatonic / A blues; prioritize bends, vibrato, and space.
songs.mustKnow: Chord form and tasteful lead phrasing.
songs.fallback: Chord support plus 2–3 recurring melodic licks.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Am I taking any solo space, or just filling behind vocals?

source.Scale Hint: A minor pentatonic / A blues; prioritize bends, vibrato, and space.
source.Must Know: Chord form and tasteful lead phrasing.
source.Fallback: Chord support plus 2–3 recurring melodic licks.
source.Rehearsal Question: Am I taking any solo space, or just filling behind vocals?

songs.title: Pride and Joy
songs.scaleHint: E blues / E minor pentatonic, with major-blues flavor. Rhythm shuffle is the real homework.
songs.mustKnow: Texas shuffle feel before flashy licks.
songs.fallback: Simplified shuffle, sparse fills.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Are we doing SRV Eb or standard? Who carries the shuffle?

source.Tuning: Eb original; standard backing versions exist

songs.recordingNote: Eb original; standard backing versions exist

songs.title: Zombie
songs.scaleHint: E minor pentatonic can work, but song needs big chord dynamics more than lead.
songs.mustKnow: Em-C-G-D progression and clean/dirt dynamic lift.
songs.fallback: Simple open chords or power chords.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Clean intro then distortion chorus, or one tone throughout?

source.Scale Hint: E minor pentatonic can work, but song needs big chord dynamics more than lead.

songs.title: Hunger Strike
songs.practiceStyle: Some improv, but learn key hooks
songs.scaleHint: D major / B minor pentatonic area for fills; stay behind vocals.
songs.mustKnow: Form and vocal-section dynamics.
songs.fallback: Chord texture and big chorus support.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Which vocalist cues the changes? Are guitar fills wanted?

songs.title: S.O.B.
songs.linkQuality: No strong direct match found; search link
songs.scaleHint: G-ish blues/major pentatonic depending arrangement; do not overplay.
songs.pentatonicBox: G minor box 1 at 3rd: 3-6 / 3-5 / 3-5 / 3-5 / 3-6 / 3-6; try G major flavor too
songs.mustKnow: Stomp/clap pocket and repeated ending.
songs.fallback: Muted/percussive chord hits.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Do they need guitar at all, or mostly vocals/percussion?

songs.title: Save a Horse (Ride a Cowboy)

songs.mustKnow: Groove and party-song form.
songs.fallback: Simple chord chucks on strong beats.

songs.title: Sweet Home Alabama
songs.scaleHint: D major pentatonic and G major pentatonic are friendly; minor blues licks also appear in Southern rock language.
songs.pentatonicBox: B minor box 1 at 7th for D major pentatonic: 7-10 / 7-9 / 7-9 / 7-9 / 7-10 / 7-10
songs.mustKnow: D-C-G riff and turnaround.
songs.fallback: Strum D-C-G and add simple fills between vocals.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Am I playing the signature riff, fills, or just rhythm?

source.Scale Hint: D major pentatonic and G major pentatonic are friendly; minor blues licks also appear in Southern rock language.

source.Must Know: D-C-G riff and turnaround.
source.Fallback: Strum D-C-G and add simple fills between vocals.
source.Rehearsal Question: Am I playing the signature riff, fills, or just rhythm?

songs.title: Dani California
songs.scaleHint: A minor pentatonic / A blues; Hendrix-ish bends and repeated motifs.
songs.mustKnow: Main riff and chorus rhythm.
songs.fallback: Play riff in verses, simple power/chord support in chorus.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Is anyone covering the solo, or can it be loose?

songs.title: Teenage Dirtbag
songs.scaleHint: E major/C# minor area; improv not the main value.
songs.mustKnow: Dynamics from quiet verse to big chorus.
songs.fallback: Simple chord strums and power chords.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Are vocals doing the energy lift, or should guitar get bigger?

songs.title: The Pretender
songs.scaleHint: A minor/F major-ish rock vocabulary, but exact rhythm hits matter more.
songs.mustKnow: Quiet intro to explosive heavy entrance.
songs.fallback: Drop-D root power-chord rhythm.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Are we definitely using Drop D?

songs.title: Here It Goes Again
songs.scaleHint: C/G major pentatonic territory; learn the arrangement instead.
songs.pentatonicBox: A minor box 1 at 5th for C major: 5-8 / 5-7 / 5-7 / 5-7 / 5-8 / 5-8
songs.mustKnow: Tight pop-rock rhythm and stops.
songs.fallback: Chord chart + steady eighths.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Is this close to the record or changed for live band?

songs.title: Purple Rain
songs.linkQuality: Excellent guitar backing track with vocal
songs.scaleHint: Bb major pentatonic / G minor pentatonic is common for original key; many jam tracks are in A or B, so match the track.

songs.mustKnow: Slow emotional bends; less is more.
songs.fallback: Big clean chords and 3–4 melodic licks.
songs.rehearsalNotes: What key are they singing it in?

songs.title: Banditos
songs.linkQuality: No strong direct match found; search link
songs.scaleHint: E/A country-rock pentatonic territory; find the root against the track and keep phrases short.

songs.mustKnow: Bar-band groove and simple lead fills.
songs.fallback: Open chords + twangy pentatonic fills.
songs.rehearsalNotes: Do they want the solo/fills or just rhythm?

references.Notes: Official help says playlists are created on desktop from My Tabs > Playlists, and songs are added from Favorites using the + button.
Page prompts sign-up to create playlists; no bulk CSV import found.
Plus/full access includes slow down, loop, solo mode, and playing along mode.
Songsterr advertises over 1 million guitar, bass, and drum tabs and AI tab creation from YouTube links.
